formations_blocks: Norway deceptive pressing block / staggered press
Norway temporarily off the press into a more passive defensive shape
adjustments: Norway begin with a baiting press on the goalkeeper then spring forward once the pass goes to the defender on his left
Norway briefly call off the press and allow Iraq to circulate the ball
Iraq consistently go direct from the start
key_matchups: Norwegian forwards vs Iraq's build-up after the goalkeeper's pass
Sørloth vs Iraqi defender after the pushing incident
phases.summary: The opening phase is defined by Iraq’s direct, long-ball approach versus Norway’s more nuanced pressing scheme. Norway initially invite the goalkeeper into possession before triggering pressure on the next pass, while Iraq also create early set-piece danger and several fouls/disruptions break up rhythm. The clearest open-play chance comes when Al-Hamadi turns Heggem, but Norway’s defender recovers enough to force a rushed shot over the bar.

formations_blocks: Norway slow play at the back with possession
Iraq pressing high and trying to win the ball back quickly
Both teams willing to go direct
Norway in a possession-dominant attacking phase, but with limited clear penetration
key_matchups: Aursnes under pressure from Iraq's continued play
Haaland vs Iraq defending on set pieces
Sørloth vs Iraq on Norway's free kick delivery
phases.summary: The phase is defined by Norway taking more of the ball and trying to build from the back, while Iraq respond with an aggressive press that repeatedly interrupts that rhythm. Despite Norway’s territorial and possession advantage, much of the football is direct and scrappy, with set pieces and second balls shaping the danger rather than sustained combination play. The key momentum swing comes through Haaland’s finishing, first putting Norway ahead and then restoring the lead shortly after Iraq’s equalizer.

adjustments: Iraq and Norway both appear to have defensive issues, with the first half described as a work in progress defensively
The commentary suggests Solbakken may prioritize tightening the defense while otherwise allowing the attacking talent to attack
phases.summary: The halftime discussion frames the match as one between two flawed teams, with both defenses looking unreliable and the game therefore open and entertaining. Norway’s big tactical question is how to balance a potentially more conservative defensive approach under Solbakken with the freedom of a Haaland-Sørloth attacking partnership.

formations_blocks: Iraq pressing aggressively and forcing Norway into turnovers
Norway struggling to play through the press and hold possession
adjustments: Iraq continue to attack on the break and from set pieces after halftime
Iraq make substitutions at 59' with Iqbal and Farji replacing Ismael and Al-Hamadi
key_matchups: Al-Hamadi vs the Norwegian back line in a 1v1 breakaway
Norwegian back line vs Iraq's through ball
Ajer in a brief confrontation with an Iraqi counterpart after the corner
phases.summary: This phase is defined by Iraq’s energetic press, which repeatedly disrupts Norway’s possession and creates direct chances in transition. Iraq come closest to scoring through a series of attacks behind the Norwegian back line, including a one-on-one for Al-Hamadi and a shot off the far post, before making changes up front at 59'.

formations_blocks: Iraq in a passive, deep defensive block for much of the closing stages
Norway in settled possession, controlling the game in the attacking half
Iraq occasionally attempting a hurried counter or long clearance rather than sustained pressure
adjustments: Thorstvedt comes on and immediately provides an attacking threat from the edge of the box
Norway’s final substitution: Berg replaces captain Ødegaard, with Haaland taking the armband
Iraq increasingly resort to desperate direct balls upfield as fatigue sets in
key_matchups: Tahseen vs Haaland: Tahseen pulls Haaland back to stop a Norwegian attack
Haaland vs Hassan: Hassan makes a brave close-range stop after a giveaway to Haaland
Norway’s aerial challenge advantage vs Iraq’s back line on the Østigård goal
phases.summary: Norway’s late control was built on possession, territorial pressure, and set-piece/aerial threat, even though Iraq had actually shown some brief signs of life before the decisive goal. Østigård’s 76th-minute strike and the late own goal turned the final stretch into a comfortable closing phase for Norway, while Iraq faded into a low-energy, reactive block and largely failed to sustain counters.
